theme: Etch a phase plate to project an image
title: Two-Plane Phase Plate: One Etched Mask, Two Fresnel Focal Images
strategies: single-shot inverse: average the two targets in target space, treat sqrt(target) as the desired focal amplitude, one inverse Fresnel propagation through ONE plane's operator, read off the phase (ignores the fixed-amplitude aperture constraint and the other plane entirely)

two-plane Gerchberg-Saxton: alternately propagate to plane A / impose sqrt(target_A) amplitude / propagate back / impose the fixed uniform aperture amplitude, then repeat for plane B using the updated mask -- iterated alternating projection onto both constraint sets
multi-restart GS with best-iterate tracking (guards against stalls/cycles)
direct local search / simulated annealing over the phase mask using the checker's own objective as a black-box score
